_note: Hand-curated expected cultural-origin distributions Q(c) for Part 3 (CBS). Curated to art-historical TRUTH, not to a diversity target: this is a Renaissance collection, so high Italian/Iberian mass is usually CORRECT. Distributions reflect influences attested in the museum's own descriptions + standard art history. Edit via PR; keep each row summing to 1.0 and titles matching the Excel inventory exactly. Validate with: python -m eval.cultural_groundtruth --validate

artworks.title: Vespasià
artworks.source: Museum description; Italian marble relief of a Roman emperor for Miquel Mai's Barcelona house.
artworks.rationale: Carved in Italy in the Roman-imperial classical idiom; minor Iberian context via its Barcelona patron Miquel Mai.

artworks.title: Trajà
artworks.source: Museum description; acquired by humanist Miquel Mai in Italy (Rome, from 1528).
artworks.rationale: Italian classical-revival marble medallion; Iberian thread only through its Catalan collector.

artworks.title: Faustina Maior [?]
artworks.source: Museum description; Roman empress relief, Mai collection.
artworks.rationale: Italian antique-revival relief reviving a Roman imperial portrait type.

artworks.title: Arqueta
artworks.source: Museum description; Northern-Italian pastiglia casket, 15th-16th c., with Diana/Actaeon myth.
artworks.rationale: A Northern-Italian luxury type with classical-mythological decoration; squarely Italian-Western.

artworks.title: Carles V adult i la batalla de Mülberg
artworks.source: Museum description; medal by the Italian sculptor Leone Leoni, Roman-imperial guise.
artworks.rationale: Italian medallist working in a Roman-imperial style for the Habsburg (Spanish) emperor; the empire reaches the Low Countries.

artworks.title: Trattato della pittura
artworks.source: Museum description; compilation of Leonardo da Vinci's notes on painting.
artworks.rationale: The Italian-Renaissance theory of painting in its purest form (the 'Florentine genius').

artworks.title: Felip II. Commemoració de l'abdicació de Carles V
artworks.source: Museum description; medal by Giacomo da Trezzo (Italian) for the Spanish Habsburg court.
artworks.rationale: Italian medallist; Iberian (Spanish Habsburg) subject and patronage; explicit Low-Countries governance thread.

artworks.title: Mare de Déu amb el Nen ("Madonna di Trapani")
artworks.source: Museum description; Sicilian (Trapani) Virgin-and-Child type with its own iconography.
artworks.rationale: Sicilian/Italian devotional type whose tender Virgin-and-Child iconography descends from Byzantine/Eastern-Christian icon traditions transmitted across the Mediterranean.

artworks.title: Davallament
artworks.source: Museum description; anonymous painter active in Valencia, 1450-1460.
artworks.rationale: Valencian production, but the meticulous detail and atmospheric landscape are strongly Flemish/Netherlandish in technique.

artworks.title: Flagel·lació de Crist
artworks.source: Museum description; from Santa Maria del Pi, Barcelona (Catalonia), 1585.
artworks.rationale: A Catalan altar front; its 'classical' architectural setting carries a moderate Italian-Renaissance influence.

artworks.title: Pilastra de balustrada
artworks.source: Museum description; baluster from a Barcelona house; the form originates in Venetian/Veronese palaces.
artworks.rationale: A Catalan architectural fragment whose very form (the baluster) is an Italian-Renaissance import; the Horace inscription reinforces the classical thread.

artworks.title: Copa de vidre
artworks.source: Museum description; Barcelona glass, c.1590, with lacticini (latticino) threadwork.
artworks.rationale: Barcelona was a major glass centre; the lacticini/latticino white-thread technique is of Venetian (Italian) origin.

artworks.title: Crist amb la Creu
artworks.source: Museum description; circle of Luis de Morales, Spanish painter from Badajoz.
artworks.rationale: A Spanish (Iberian) painter, but using the sfumato pioneered by Leonardo da Vinci - an explicit Italian-Renaissance technical debt.

artworks.title: Retrat de Carles V
artworks.source: Museum description; anonymous Madrid painter, likely painted in Spain; Spanish court fashion.
artworks.rationale: Spanish portrait of the emperor in Spanish dress; the small-portrait-for-relatives format carries a faint Netherlandish-court echo.

artworks.title: Caixa amb calaixos. L'Anunciació
artworks.source: Museum description; a furniture type typical of Catalonia, 15th-18th c.
artworks.rationale: Explicitly a Catalan vernacular furniture type; the painted Annunciation is conventional devotional content.

artworks.title: Gerro de ceràmica
artworks.source: Museum description: 'until the 16th c. Spanish ceramics were heavily influenced by the Arab tradition'; Talavera de la Reina.
artworks.rationale: A Castilian (Talavera) production whose ceramic tradition is, by the museum's own text, deeply shaped by Arab/Islamic-Mediterranean craft.

artworks.title: Plat de ceràmica
artworks.source: Museum description; Manises (Valencia) lustreware with golden/blue reflejos.
artworks.rationale: Hispano-Moresque Manises lustreware: the golden-reflection (reflejo metalico) technique is a direct inheritance of al-Andalus Islamic ceramics.

artworks.title: Casc morrión
artworks.source: Museum description; a helmet popular in 16th-c. Hispanic armies; classicist relief of Mars and Venus.
artworks.rationale: A Spanish (Hispanic) military helmet whose refined relief decoration is classicist and mythological - a moderate Italian-Western thread.

artworks.title: La Visitació
artworks.source: Museum description; 'of Flemish school', late 15th c.
artworks.rationale: Explicitly Flemish-school panel painting; interior-with-windows setting is a Netherlandish convention.

artworks.title: Anunciació
artworks.source: Museum description; pendant to 'La Visitació', same Flemish school, size and style.
artworks.rationale: Companion Flemish-school panel; same Netherlandish idiom.

artworks.title: Mare de Déu de la Llet
artworks.source: Museum description; 'follower of Hans Memling', c.1500.
artworks.rationale: In the manner of the Bruges master Memling - strongly Flemish; the Council-of-Trent context gives a faint local-reception thread.

artworks.title: Santa Maria Magdalena
artworks.source: Museum description; 'Master of the Parrot', a Flemish painter active in Antwerp, early 16th c.
artworks.rationale: Antwerp Flemish painter; the detailed objects/textures in an intimate setting are hallmark Netherlandish.

artworks.title: Retrat de cavaller de l'orde de sant Jaume
artworks.source: Museum description; Anthonis Mor (Utrecht), Habsburg court portraitist; sitter wears the Order of St James.
artworks.rationale: A Netherlandish master, but the sober court-portrait mode and the Spanish military order place a strong Iberian-Habsburg thread.

artworks.title: Triomf del Temps sobre la Fama
artworks.source: Museum description; workshop of Willem Dermoyen (Flemish tapestry); illustrates Petrarch's Trionfi.
artworks.rationale: A Flemish (Brussels) tapestry workshop, but the subject is Petrarch's Italian humanist poem - a large Italian-Western literary thread; acquired for the Palau de la Generalitat (local thread).

artworks.title: L’Anunciació
artworks.source: Engraving by Albrecht Dürer, 1504 (German Renaissance).
artworks.rationale: Quintessential German-Renaissance printmaking; Durer's documented contact with Italian masters justifies a modest Italian thread.

artworks.title: La resurrecció de Llàtzer
artworks.source: Engraving by Lucas Kilian, German engraver (Augsburg) trained in Venice.
artworks.rationale: German engraver; his Venetian training gives a real Italian-Western component.

artworks.title: Rellotge de butxaca
artworks.source: Museum description; the first portable pocket watches were invented and built in Germany in the 16th c.
artworks.rationale: By the museum's own text, German horological invention - firmly Northern-European.

artworks.title: Moisès
artworks.source: Engraving by Jacob Matham (Dutch/Haarlem engraver, Goltzius circle). No museum description text.
artworks.rationale: Northern (Dutch) printmaking; faint Italian thread typical of the mannerist print culture. Low confidence (no description).

artworks.title: El Judici Final
artworks.source: Engraving by Jan Wierix (Flemish engraver, Antwerp). No museum description text.
artworks.rationale: Flemish/Netherlandish printmaking. Low confidence (no description).

artworks.title: Enric IV, rei de França
artworks.source: Engraving by Thomas de Leu, French engraver of Flemish origin. No museum description text.
artworks.rationale: French/Flemish portrait engraving - Northern-European. Low confidence (no description).

artworks.title: Caterina de Mèdici
artworks.source: Engraving by Thomas de Leu; sitter is Catherine de' Medici (Italian-born queen of France).
artworks.rationale: Northern-European engraving of an Italian-born (Medici) French queen - a genuine Italian thread via the sitter. Low confidence (no description).

artworks.title: Joana d’Albret
artworks.source: Engraving by Thomas de Leu; Jeanne d'Albret, queen of Navarre. No museum description text.
artworks.rationale: French portrait engraving - Northern-European. Low confidence (no description).

artworks.title: María de Médici
artworks.source: Engraving by Thomas de Leu; Marie de' Medici (Italian-born queen of France).
artworks.rationale: Northern-European engraving of an Italian-born (Medici) French queen. Low confidence (no description).
